answer: Argon2id with parameters: 64MB memory cost, 3 iterations, 4 parallelism factor.
Refresh token expiration is 7 days with rotation policy (rotate on use).
The Notification Service runs on port 9200.
RDS PostgreSQL 15.4 is currently running, upgraded from PostgreSQL 14.9.
API Gateway throttle limits: 5000 burst per second, 2000 steady-state requests per second.
Argon2id was chosen over bcrypt because it is resistant to side-channel attacks, providing stronger security guarantees in multi-tenant environments.
Drizzle is used instead of Prisma because it has zero external dependencies, reducing attack surface and improving supply chain security.
Both Portfolio and Notification services verify auth tokens using Lambda-JWKS endpoint to validate RS256-signed JWTs.
Rate limiting at ALB level (1000 req/min per IP) provides DDoS protection before traffic reaches application containers, reducing resource consumption and container scaling needs.
Request > Gateway HTTP-API > Lambda-JWKS-claims validation > VPCLink > ALB (rate limit check) > handlePostLogin > Auth Service (RBAC role-hierarchy validation) > Portfolio Service.

If JWKS endpoint fails, all services cannot validate new tokens. Auth Service has an LRU cache (1000 keys, 5 min TTL) as grace period. If issue continues, private key stored locally is used. All services fail-down together.
Real-time portfolio alerting depends on WebSocket support, which is planned for Q3. Current status: NOT IN CONTEXT (system under investigation).
Yes, UTC+14 token expiry bug has been fixed by enforcing UTC timezone for all token timestamp operations (unix-ts format) across the system.

Implement webhook endpoint using: Notification Service (Go/Chi:9200) with exponential backoff retry (5 attempts over 24 hours), MJML templating for auth event emails, SES/SNS for delivery, and webhook table with exp-backoff tracking.
With connection pool size of 20 per instance × 5 Auth Service instances = 100 maximum simultaneous database connections.

During tax-lot processing, Celery/Redis has unbounded memory usage issue (currently under investigation). Connection pool (20 connections) handles DB access, but Celery memory leak can cause pod eviction and loss of in-flight jobs.

No, Fargate Spot cannot be used for Auth Service. Rule R4 specifies Spot-ONLY-batch: Spot instances are only for batch jobs. Auth Service requires guaranteed availability with min 2 instances across 3 AZs.
Rule R3 prohibits adding in-memory session cache: all services require statelessness to enable horizontal scaling. JWKS-LRU cache is exception for cryptographic key lookup, not session data.
30-day backup retention exceeds RPO (1 hour) and RTO (4 hours) targets. 1-hour RPO ensures data loss < 1 hour; 4-hour RTO enables recovery in us-west2 failover within 4 hours.
Connection string changed to require sslmode=verify-full with global-bundle certificate validation (RDS 15.4 upgrade from 14.9).
tax_lots table columns: id, account_id, security_id, acquisition_date, cost_basis, quantity, adjusted, created_at.

MFA affects login flow by adding TOTP verification step: handlePostLogin > Argon2id password verify > MFA-TOTP prompt > user enters code from 10-code single-use backup > RS256 access token (15m) + refresh token (7d).
10 single-use recovery codes are generated during MFA setup. When a code is used, it becomes invalid. If all codes depleted, user must re-enable MFA with new QR code.
Webhook retry policy: exponential backoff with 5 attempts over 24 hours. Soft limit: 10 notifications/hour per webhook.

Security posture: At rest—PII encrypted with AES256-GCM per tenant. Passwords hashed with Argon2id (64M/3/4). In transit—mTLS via Envoy between services, sslmode=verify-full to RDS with global SSL bundle, RS256-JWKS for API auth. Key rotation every 90 days. Secrets stored externally (NOT IN CONTEXT: specific secret manager).

Full login lifecycle: Request > API Gateway (rate limit 1000/min/IP) > ALB > Lambda-JWKS-claims (validates auth context) > handlePostLogin route > Argon2id password verification > MFA-TOTP prompt > 10 recovery codes validated (single-use) > RS256 access token issued (15m expiry) > RS256 refresh token issued (7d expiry, rotate on use) > Response with tokens via mTLS > Client stores in secure storage.

New Billing Service (Rust): Must follow handle{Verb}{Resource} pattern, use RFC7807 error responses with traceId, integrate with Auth Service for RS256-JWKS token validation, implement connection pooling (pool20), participate in Envoy mTLS service mesh, use Pino-JSON-AsyncLocal logging, support ClaudeCode-review in CI/CD, deploy to ECS-Fargate (min 2 instances, 3 AZs), must handle 1MB ALB payload limit.

1500 req/min from single IP: ALB rate limiter rejects after 1000 req/min (Rule R2), returns 429 Too Many Requests. Subsequent requests dropped at ALB > not reaching app-rate limiter > not consuming container resources > blue-green deployment unaffected.

JWT signing key dependencies: Auth Service (generates/rotates RS256) > JWKS endpoint (publishes public key) > Lambda-JWKS (validates tokens) > API Gateway (claims extraction) > all services (Auth, Portfolio, Notification validate via JWKS) > LRU cache (1000/5min) > fallback to local private key if JWKS down.

Deployment pipeline: Commit > GitHub Actions (lint > testcontainers > unit tests > build Docker image) > ECR push > ECS task definition update > blue-green deployment via ALB target group swap > zero-downtime cutover > ClaudeCode-review gate before production > Spot-ONLY batch jobs for async tasks > RTO 4 hours with us-west2 failover if us-east-1 primary fails.
